titleStmt.title: Letter from Major C. Harold Heathcote to James Ryan, 19 September 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Major C. Harold Heathcote

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Major C. Heathcote to James Ryan (1891-1970). The letter is in reference to Ryan's attempt to secure the return of items taken from him after his arrest following the Rising.James Ryan studied medicine in UCD. He was appointed chief medical officer in the GPO during the Easter Rising. After the Rising he was imprisoned in Stafford and, later, Frongoch and was released in August 1916. Ryan was later a founder member of Fianna Fáil and a government minister. Heathcote was officer in charge of prisoners' effects in Richmond Barracks, where most of those arrested after the Rising was initially held.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Prisoners' Effects.
James Ryan.
Claim No. 90. James Ryan, Tomcoole. Taghmon, Co. Wexford. Reference to your letter of 18/9/16. There is no occasion to return. It will be quite
satisfactory if you can call before the end of the
month. C. Harold Heathcote
Major.
0. i/c Prisoners' Effects.
Richmond Barracks,
Dublin.
19/9/16.
